title: Startup PhysicsX Hits $2.4 Billion Valuation to Provide AI for Manufacturing
summary: PhysicsX, a British startup that develops artificial intelligence models for manufacturing components like jet engines and semiconductors, has raised $300 million at a valuation of roughly $2.4 billion, competing in a crowded field that includes Amazon.com Inc founder Jeff Bezos.

title: Carlyle Buys Korea Home Appliance Rental Firm for $700 Million
summary: Carlyle Group Inc. agreed to acquire Chung Ho Group, a South Korean home and health-care appliance rental platform, for $700 million, as rising inheritance-tax burdens in the country push more owners to sell, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Corporate Japan Faces Ratings Risk as It Ramps Up Borrowing
summary: Japanese businesses are ramping up borrowing to cover cash shortfalls fueled by record merger activity, booming capital investments and mounting investor pressure for shareholder returns, putting their credit ratings at risk.

title: Intesa Sanpaolo Board Said to Meet to Review Monte Paschi Bid
summary: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA is preparing a bid for Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA, hours after rival Banco BPM SpA proposed a €50 billion ($58 billion) tie-up, according to people familiar with the matter, injecting fresh momentum into a two-year consolidation push that’s transforming Italy’s finance industry.

title: Air France-KLM Keeps Door Ajar on EasyJet Role as Bid Looms
summary: Air France KLM Chief Executive Officer Ben Smith signaled he’s willing to consider a possible involvement in a bid for EasyJet Plc, saying he’d take a call from suitor Castlelake LP should the investment firm come with a proposition.

title: Banco BPM Proposes Monte Paschi Deal for €50 Billion Bank
summary: Banco BPM SpA has proposed a merger of “equals” with Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena Spa that would create Italy’s second-largest banking group, with a market value of more than €50 billion.

title: Oil Jumps as Iran and Israel Exchange Fire With Truce at Risk
summary: Oil surged after Iran and Israel exchanged fire, threatening a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East as talks to end the war falter.

title: Stock Losses Build as AI Rally Cools, Oil Jumps: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks and bonds dropped as investors faced a trio of headwinds with a pullback in the artificial-intelligence trade, mounting bets on a US interest rate hike and rising oil prices due to a worsening of the Middle East conflict.

title: IMF Chief: We Can't Repeat the Mistakes of Globalization With AI
summary: International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva has spent the past six years navigating some of the biggest challenges facing the global economy — from the Covid-19 pandemic and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine to the rapid emergence of artificial intelligence. In this episode of Leaders With Francine Lacqua, Georgieva discusses what it takes to lead an institution representing 191 countries, how the IMF approaches economic crises and why trust is essential when governments are asked to make difficult reforms. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Danantara to Start US Bond Roadshow as Indonesia Rout Deepens
summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is poised to kick off the US leg of its planned dollar bond roadshow this week, as the nation’s policymakers step up efforts to halt a deepening selloff in the nation’s markets.

title: Goldman Sees Rupee Weakness Capped After Steps to Boost Inflows
summary: The Indian rupee’s slide may be nearing a floor after authorities unveiled measures to boost foreign inflows, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

title: Stocks Tumble as AI Trades Unwind | The Asia Trade 6/8/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB Risks Repeating 2011 Mistake With Rate Hike, Economists Warn
summary: The European Central Bank’s resolve to uphold its inflation-quashing reputation risks luring it into a damaging error when it meets this week, economists warn.

title: Indonesia Market Rout Deepens as Bonds, Currency, Stocks Slide
summary: The rout in Indonesian markets deepened on Monday with the nation’s bonds, stocks and currency tumbling.

title: ‘Odd Lots’ Meets ‘The China Show’: Stock Rout Hits Asia
summary: Asian stocks dropped Monday as AI-related shares sold off after a blistering rally, led by losses in Korean chipmakers SK Hynix and Samsung. Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway from Bloomberg’s Odd Lots podcast join Yvonne Man and Stephen Engle on The China Show. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Altitude Sickness Brings Stocks Down — Hard
summary: A raft of IPOs led by Elon Musk’s SpaceX threatens to intensify the condition.

title: Saudis Lower July Oil Prices, Though Still at Decades High
summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade to Asia for a second straight month, though the premium for barrels to the kingdom’s largest market remained near the highest in decades.

title: Korean Stocks Tumble as Unwinding AI Trades Threaten Bull Run
summary: South Korean stocks plunged, imperiling the world’s biggest rally, as investors pulled back from artificial intelligence bets that have fueled the global bull market in equities.

title: Treasuries Drop as Jobs Data, Iran Tensions Fuel Rate Hike Bets
summary: Treasuries fell as investors ramped up bets that the Federal Reserve will need to raise interest rates, while escalating tensions in the Middle East added to inflation concerns.

title: Flood Warnings for South China Farmlands Raise Rice-Crop Risks
summary: Forecasts for heavy rains in southern China over the next week are raising the risk of flash floods, potentially inundating crops and damaging rice fields.

title: Copper Rebounds From Friday Slump on China-Buying and US Flows
summary: Copper edged up to recoup some of last week’s losses as buying activity in China and flows of metal to the US supported the demand outlook.

title: Goldman’s Japan Chief Counts on New Leaders for Growth
summary: Goldman Sachs Japan president Hidehiro Imatsu is promoting younger executives at the firm as a merger boom, rising stocks and volatile bond yields fuel the most intense talent war in years.

title: Nvidia CEO Says Selloff in Tech Stocks Is a Buying Opportunity
summary: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang called a global tech stocks selloff that began last week a buying opportunity, saying the buildout of artificial intelligence has just begun.

title: Indonesia’s Foreign Reserves Suffer Longest Decline Since 2018
summary: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell for a fifth straight month in May, underscoring the cost of policymakers’ efforts to steady the rupiah after its slide to a record low.

title: Goldman’s Moe Sees Korea Stock Rebound After ‘Scary’ Correction
summary: South Korean stocks are expected to bounce back after a drop that triggered a circuit breaker, said Timothy Moe, Goldman Sachs chief Asia-Pacific regional equity strategist.

title: China Coking Coal Hits Highest Since 2024 on Safety Shutdowns
summary: Chinese coking coal rose to the highest since 2024 as the aftermath of a deadly mining accident and ongoing safety inspections kept prices elevated.

title: Japanese Pension Fund Proxies Buy Record Foreign Bonds in May
summary: Proxies for Japanese pension funds bought a record amount of overseas bonds last month, adding to signs of robust demand for foreign debt even as local yields climbed.

title: Gold Extends Loss as Israel-Iran Clashes Endanger Mideast Truce
summary: Gold extended a decline after Israel said it struck Iranian targets in response to missile attacks by Tehran, threatening a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East.

title: Asia Coal Prices Rise to 22-Month High on Indonesia Export Rules
summary: A key coal benchmark for Asia rose to the highest level in nearly two years as Indonesia’s new export rules delayed shipments, tightening supplies just as demand for the power-plant fuel rises with the onset of summer.

title: Japan’s Nikkei Slumps as Tech Selloff, Iran Tensions Deepen
summary: Japanese stocks fell by the most since March as AI shares retreated from their highs amid rising expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer following strong US jobs data.

title: Goldman’s Moe on the Slump in South Korea Stocks
summary: Timothy Moe, Goldman Sachs chief APAC regional equity strategist, says he expects South Korean stocks to bounce back after the drop that triggered a circuit breaker. He speaks with with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Shery Ahn on Bloomberg. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Korea Unveils Measures to Stem Won Slide, Curb Speculation
summary: South Korea unveiled targeted steps to support the won after it fell to the weakest level since 2009, pledging firm action against speculative trading and other activities seen as amplifying recent volatility.

title: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel as Trump Defends Ceasefire
summary: Iran fired several rounds of missiles toward Israel, as President Donald Trump pushed to preserve a faltering ceasefire in the US’s 100-day conflict with Tehran. Bloomberg's Laura Davison breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan’s Growth Holds Up Despite Drop in Business Investment
summary: Japan’s economy grew at a still solid pace at the start of the year even after the turbulence in Iran prompted businesses to cut investment.

title: Air New Zealand Steps Up Flight Cuts as Fuel Costs Mount
summary: Air New Zealand will cut more flights in the three months through October as it faces rising jet fuel costs and soft demand, particularly in its home market, Chief Executive Officer Nikhil Ravishankar said.
